dimensions.label: Excessive concurrent work
matchedGroups: priority conflict / priority overload / competing priorities / focus fragmentation / execution demand exceeds
sequence work / reduce active work / reduce concurrent / protect highest-leverage work
missingGroups: concurrent work / too many projects / too much work in progress / work in progress / wip
dimensions.rationale: The primary operating constraint is excessive concurrent work, which fragments focus and exceeds organizational execution capacity.

dimensions.label: Weak decision rights
matchedGroups: decision rights / decision authority / unclear governance / governance friction
approval bottleneck / approval dependency / centralized authority / leadership dependency
clarify decision / distribute authority / reduce approval
dimensions.rationale: Unclear decision rights and approval dependency slow execution and push routine decisions upward.

dimensions.label: Acquisition fragmentation
matchedGroups: fragmentation / incompatible process / systems integration / operating model ambiguity / knowledge continuity
integration failure / organizational continuity failure / continuity failure
dimensions.rationale: The Vector acquisition amplified fragmentation across systems, operating practices, knowledge, and leadership.

dimensions.label: Staffing is not the root cause
matchedGroups: capacity constraint / execution capacity / resource constraint
governance friction / priority conflict / coordination breakdown / decision latency
missingGroups: without adding headcount / avoid additional headcount / staffing is not / not a staffing / hiring will not
dimensions.rationale: The apparent capacity problem is driven primarily by prioritization, governance, coordination, and concurrency rather than insufficient staffing.

dimensions.label: Customer escalation is downstream
matchedGroups: customer friction / customer escalation / strategic account risk / delivery failure
downstream / propagate / caused by / reinforced by / execution capacity / coordination system
dimensions.rationale: Customer escalations are an important consequence of operating-system failures, but not the primary root cause.

falsePositives.label: Recommends headcount as the primary intervention
Recommends major technology replacement without causal support
Recommends leadership removal without causal support
Recommends broad reorganization as the primary intervention

conclusion: Discovery identified most of the true organizational explanation, with material gaps remaining.
